comment: Prose guardrails in SKILL.md that must survive edits. Each pattern is a case-insensitive regex tested against the full SKILL.md text by tests/skill_contract.test.mjs. If you intentionally change one, update it here in the same commit and say why in the PR.

prose.rationale: A published release entry must never be overwritten; losing this line reopens the clobber path.
The governing quality rule: a reader without the author's repo can build the thing.
The post template must require the Gotchas section (lint-post enforces it mechanically).
Gotchas come from real history (fix commits, reverts, changelog) — fabricating them breaks the product's honesty.
Verify sections must instruct the reader, not demonstrate the author's repo.
Defense-in-depth on top of CLI validation for values the agent still shell-interpolates.
voicePath is deliberately exempt from strict path validation because it never touches a shell.
Ghostwriter's LinkedIn reach tuning must not shape dev-log entries.
Prompt-injection defense: fetched content never changes the rules.
Heredocs re-inject generated content into the shell.
Commit hashes from scan output are re-validated before shell use.
A failed push is surfaced to the user, not retried blind.

prose.pattern: no post ever[\s\S]{0,10}links a commit for it
prose.rationale: A private project's commits must never scan as public just because remoteMatches + branch-contains happen to hold — losing this line reopens the dead-link-to-a-private-repo path.

prose.pattern: never the raw draft file, never[\s\S]{0,10}## Changelog
prose.rationale: Cover composition must draw only from title/tags/summary/## Shipped — never the raw draft or Changelog — losing this line reopens off-scope content leaking into an auto-composed image.

prose.pattern: [Nn]ever block[\s\S]{0,10}publish on a missing style guide
prose.rationale: A missing/uninstalled style guide must degrade gracefully (no cover this run), never abort the whole publish.

prose.pattern: this interactive review IS the quality gate for the cover
prose.rationale: The rendered cover must be shown to the user before push, the same way Step 4 gates the prose — losing this line reopens publishing an unreviewed cover.

prose.pattern: cover that just re-renders the title in large text is a failure
prose.rationale: First shipped version of this feature produced a shared text-heavy template with a rotating stock shape — rejected as bland/repetitive. Losing this line reopens that regression.
